articles.title: Cebu heat index may reach 41°C as humid weather persists Friday
articles.description: LAPU-LAPU CITY, Cebu — Cebuanos can expect generally fair weather with hot and humid conditions to persist on Friday, May 15, the state weather bureau reported. ​The Visayas Pagasa Regional Services Division forecast daytime temperatures to range from 27 to 33 degrees Celsius, and evening temperatures to range from 27 to 31 degrees Celsius. READ: Explainer: Heat index levels classification However, the heat index may climb to 41 degrees Celsius on Friday, which Pagasa categorizes as “extreme caution.” The weather bureau explained that the heat index would indicate how hot it would feel when humidity would be factored in with [...]... Keep on reading: Cebu heat index may reach 41°C as humid weather persists Friday

articles.title: Giant “stealth” magma surge triggered thousands of earthquakes beneath Atlantic island
articles.description: Deep beneath Portugal’s São Jorge Island, a massive surge of magma silently pushed upward from more than 20 kilometers underground in 2022, triggering thousands of earthquakes and briefly raising fears of a volcanic eruption. Scientists discovered that the molten rock climbed astonishingly fast — enough to fill 32,000 Olympic swimming pools — before stalling just 1.6 kilometers below the surface in what researchers call a “failed eruption.”

articles.title: Eastern Visayas swelters under dangerous heat index levels
articles.description: TACLOBAN CITY — Several areas in Eastern Visayas continued to experience dangerous heat conditions on Thursday, prompting authorities to urge residents to stay hydrated and avoid prolonged sun exposure. According to the Department of Science and Technology–Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (DOST-Pagasa), Borongan City in Eastern Samar recorded the highest heat index in the region at 45 degrees Celsius, placing it under the “danger” category. READ: HEAT INDEX WATCH: 49 areas to reach ‘danger’ level on Thursday, May 14 Guiuan, also in Eastern Samar, registered a heat index of 43°C, while Maasin City in Southern Leyte reached 44°C. [...]... Keep on reading: Eastern Visayas swelters under dangerous heat index levels
